chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Spl. Criminal Bail Application No. 389 of 2025 Mola Dad vs. The State For the Applicant: Mr. Rashid Hussain, Advocate alongwith Applicant Mola Dad. Date of hearing: 31.12.2025 Date of announcement: 31.12.2025 1. For Orders on M.A. No. 19173/2025. (U/A) 2. For Orders on M.A. No. 19174/2025. (Ex/A) 3. For hearing of Bail Application. ORDER Muhammad Osman Ali Hadi, J. Urgency granted. Learned counsel undertakes to place on record certified copies, true translations etc. during the course of the week; application disposed of in terms herein. The Applicant seeks protective bail in respect of F.I.R. No. 02/2023-24, registered on 02.05.2024 before P.S. Customs Office Sukkur, pertaining to offence/s under Section/s 2(s), 32 of Customs Act, 1969 read with SRO 566/2005 dated 06.06.2025 & SRO 499(I) 2009 dated 13.06.2009 punishable under clause 8 & 89 of Section 156 of Customs Act, 1969, and 157 (i) & of the Customs Act, 1969. 2. Learned counsel submits that the applicant has been erroneously nominated in the aforementioned case and is intent upon surrendering to the Trial Court. However, in order to be enabled in such regard, the Applicant seeks to be admitted to protective bail. 3. In view of the foregoing, and without adverting to the merits of the case1, the applicant is admitted to protective bail for a period of ten days from the date hereof till end of Court hours on 10.01.2026 or upon surrender of the Applicant before the Trial Court, whichever is earlier, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Thousand Only) and a personal recognizance bond, in the like amount, to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. 4.

However, in order to be enabled in such regard, the Applicant seeks to be admitted to protective bail. 3. In view of the foregoing, and without adverting to the merits of the case1, the applicant is admitted to protective bail for a period of ten days from the date hereof till end of Court hours on 10.01.2026 or upon surrender of the Applicant before the Trial Court, whichever is earlier, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Thousand Only) and a personal recognizance bond, in the like amount, to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. 4. This Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE 1 Mukhtar Awan vs. The State reported as 1990 PCrLJ 617; Sabir Hussain vs. The State reported as 1999 MLD 2208.
